retrieved_docs.title: Pediatric Dyslipidemia -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Children with FH have a 90% lifetime risk of developing coronary artery disease. In children with FH, changes in endothelial function and carotid intima-media thickness can be detected, which are reliable markers of clinical outcomes in later life. Therefore, early detection and control of dyslipidemia are strongly recommended to reduce the risk of cardiovascular events in adulthood and also reduce morbidity and mortality. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Erectile Dysfunction -- Evaluation -- Further Testing (Optional)
retrieved_docs.content: Endothelial cell dysfunction can be tested by various means, such as the penile nitric oxide release test, peripheral arterial tonometry, and flow-mediated dilation. There are also different serum markers such as C reactive protein, endothelin 1, vascular cell adhesion molecule-1, as well as the presence of endothelial progenitor cells and microparticles. [90] None of these tests for endothelial cell function has much current clinical utility, and they are of research interest only at present.

retrieved_docs.title: Congenital Hereditary Endothelial Dystrophy -- Etiology -- Risk Factors
retrieved_docs.content: Family history positive for CHED and consanguineous parents increases a neonates’ risk of being born with the disease. It is recommended that families with children diagnosed with CHED be offered genetic counseling due to a 25% risk of recurring in future offspring. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Maturity Onset Diabetes in the Young -- Complications
retrieved_docs.content: MODY 3 (HNF1A): It carries a high risk of microvascular and macrovascular complications, similar to DM1 and DM2. [9] The HDL-C is typically elevated, which can help distinguish it from DM2. Additionally, hsCRP levels are typically lower in HNF1A than in other forms of DM, thereby making it a potential biomarker. This is despite the fact that these patients have high HDL-C and low hsCRP levels; they are particularly prone to cardiovascular disease. Thus it is imperative to start a statin in these patients by the time they are 40 years old. Pancreatic exocrine dysfunction has also been reported in this genetic subtype. [32] MODY 4 (PDX1): This rare defect has been associated with pancreatic agenesis, neonatal DM, and pancreatic exocrine dysfunction. [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension of the Newborn -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: High PVR relative to systemic vascular resistance is essential to normal intrauterine fetal circulation. Fluid-filled alveoli and hypoxia-induced pulmonary vasoconstriction in the presence of circulating vasoconstrictors such as endothelin-1 and thromboxane maintain high PVR in the fetal phase Lak. Conversely, circulating levels of vasodilators such as nitric oxide and prostaglandins are low. [9] Pulmonary reactivity to vasodilators increases with increasing gestational age.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Endothelial Derived Relaxation Factor -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Stimulators of EDRF release include insulin and adiponectin. Chronic NO release by endothelial cells can be upregulated by dietary factors, estrogen, and exercise, and downregulated by oxidative stress, pollution, smoking, and oxidized low-density lipoproteins. NO release is also decreased with aging and in vascular diseases such as hypertension and diabetes. With impaired nitric oxide production, such as by oxidative stress, spontaneous hypertension, aging, and diabetes, this exacerbates endothelium-dependent contractions, and can contribute to decreased endothelium-dependent vasodilation in patients with essential hypertension or diabetes, as well as aging patients. Endothelin 1 can also contribute to endothelial dysfunction and vascular dysfunction. Also, under conditions of hypoxia, nitric oxide can cause unbalanced activation of soluble guanylyl cyclase such that cyclic inosine monophosphate (cIMP) production increases, instead of cGMP.  This increased cIMP can then lead to contraction of underlying vascular smooth muscle, instead of relaxation. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension of the Newborn -- Pathophysiology
retrieved_docs.content: During normal transition after birth, several events occur simultaneously, which results in a smooth transition to extrauterine life. A drastic fall in pulmonary arterial pressure following the first breath accompanies increased pulmonary blood flow when the umbilical cord is clamped. The increased partial pressure of oxygen and the initiation of ventilation also stimulate the production of vasodilators such as nitric oxide and prostacyclins; cyclic guanosine monophosphate (cGMP) and cyclic adenosine monophosphate (cAMP) mediate pulmonary vasodilation through endothelial nitric oxide. [10]

retrieved_docs.title: Persistent Pulmonary Hypertension of the Newborn -- Evaluation
retrieved_docs.content: Brain natriuretic peptide (BNP) is a hormone produced by stressed right ventricles. BNP levels are elevated in babies with PPHN. BNP level of more than 550pg/ml is predictive of persistent pulmonary hypertension. [18]

retrieved_docs.title: Ankle Brachial Index -- Clinical Significance
retrieved_docs.content: Minimal variations in ABI value occur with race, sex, age, and height, but the overall predictability of peripheral artery disease (PAD) is similar among groups. [13] [14] Special considerations for the pediatric population: In early infancy, ABI values average lower values than adults (58% below 0.9); however, ABI reaches normal values after 12 months. [15] Studies support use in the pediatric population to reduce radiation exposure to more invasive tests with good reliability. [16] In general, the variation in measurements is less in young, healthy individuals.

retrieved_docs.title: Risk Factors for Coronary Artery Disease -- Clinical Significance -- Flow-Mediated Dilation (FMD) and Endothelin Function
retrieved_docs.content: FMD is another proposed test that can potentially predict cardiovascular risk by measuring the health of blood vessel endothelial function. Physiologic and pharmacologic stress, such as hypertension, smoking, or certain medications, can alter this. There are different methods to measure FMD. Protocols involving vasoactive agents via coronary catheterization is a more direct measurement of the coronary artery endothelial function, specifically referred to as coronary flow reserve (CFR). [128] Brachial artery flow-mediated dilation and reactive hyperemia-peripheral arterial tonometry (RH-PAT) are more peripheral measurements. A 2015 meta-analysis conferred that these 2 methods demonstrated similar prognostic value on cardiovascular outcomes. Additional research is necessary to determine whether this screening strategy can improve cardiovascular outcomes. [129]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 4 -- Community 2885
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), indicating that children born prematurely with low birth weight have an increased risk of developing reduced height later in life.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 29 -- Level 1 -- Community 75
retrieved_docs.content: The community’s ontology integrates a wide range of obstetric and perinatal concepts through hierarchical and associative links. “مقدمات تسمم الحمل” (precursors of pre‑eclampsia) is clinically associated with “Databases” and serves as a parent concept for the standardized queries “مضاعفات وعوامل خطر الحمل والمخاض والولادة (باستثناء حالات الإجهاض وولادة جنين ميت) (SMQ)” (pregnancy‑complication and risk‑factor SMQ) and “ارتفاع ضغط الدم (SMQ)” (hypertension SMQ); both SMQs and the “Newborn Screening Terminology” appear within the “مقدمات تسمم الحمل” subset, indicating that newborn‑screening data and blood‑pressure disorders are relevant to early identification of pre‑eclampsia risk. “مضاعفات الحمل” (pregnancy complications) is a broader class that includes “اضطراب جنيني” (fetal disorder) and specific events such as “تمزق الأغشية الباكر” (premature rupture of membranes) (ISA relationship) and “تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر” (threatened preterm labor) (inverse‑ISA relationship). The late‑effect

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1494
retrieved_docs.content: A gestational age of 29‑30 weeks completed is associated with a liveborn infant, indicating that pregnancies reaching this stage can result in a live birth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 3 -- Community 2605
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born preterm with low birth weight often develop reduced linear growth. In turn, قصر القامة is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), suggesting that short stature in this vulnerable population may be linked to an increased risk of death shortly after birth. The Pediatric Adverse Events Terminology encompasses adverse pediatric outcomes, and its subset includes the concepts تدلّي الحبل السري (umbilical cord prolapse) and وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death). Together, these relationships highlight a network of interrelated risk factors and adverse events: preterm low birth weight, short stature, and neonatal death are closely connected, while umbilical cord prolapse is also recognized within the same adverse‑event framework, underscoring the importance of comprehensive monitoring and intervention in high‑risk newborns.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 4 -- Community 2821
retrieved_docs.content: Threat of premature labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر) is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), indicating that individuals who are shorter in height may have an increased risk of experiencing preterm labor. This association highlights the importance of recognizing short stature as a potential clinical indicator when assessing pregnant patients for the likelihood of premature delivery, thereby informing monitoring strategies and preventive care measures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849
retrieved_docs.content: Poor reproductive history and elective termination of pregnancy are clinically associated with preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This relationship indicates that women who have experienced a suboptimal reproductive history or who have undergone an elective termination of pregnancy may be at increased risk for developing preterm labor. Recognizing these associations can help clinicians identify patients who require closer monitoring and targeted interventions to prevent or manage preterm labor, thereby improving maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2825
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born preterm with low birth weight often experience growth restriction that results in reduced adult height. In turn, قصر القامة is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), suggesting that short stature in this context reflects severe underlying health challenges that increase the risk of death shortly after birth. Recognizing the link between low‑birth‑weight preterm infants, subsequent short stature, and heightened neonatal mortality can guide clinicians in early monitoring and intervention strategies to improve outcomes for this vulnerable population.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 20 -- Level 2 -- Community 763
retrieved_docs.content: and risk factors of pre‑eclampsia, placental disorders, amniotic‑fluid deficiencies, uterine contractile events, and labor‑induction failures with both uncomplicated (normal) and high‑risk obstetric scenarios, including singleton and multiple gestations. Recognizing these interrelationships supports more precise risk stratification, diagnosis, and management of pregnant patients across the full spectrum of obstetric care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1449
retrieved_docs.content: Preterm low‑birth‑weight infants are clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature). Short stature itself is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), indicating that children who remain short may have an increased risk of dying in the newborn period. Additionally, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is clinically associated with قصر القامة, suggesting that pregnancies complicated by early labor threats often result in offspring who develop short stature. Collectively, these relationships highlight short stature as a common clinical link among preterm low birth weight, threatened preterm labor, and adverse neonatal outcomes such as neonatal death, underscoring its relevance for risk assessment and early intervention in perinatal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2316
retrieved_docs.content: A poor reproductive history, reflecting previous obstetric complications or adverse outcomes, is clinically associated with an increased risk of preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This connection highlights the importance of detailed reproductive assessments in prenatal care to identify women who may be more likely to experience early delivery and to apply targeted monitoring and preventive strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 3 -- Community 2301
retrieved_docs.content: Gestation abnormality and pregnancy and puerperal disorders are both linked to the condition ولادة قبل الأوان (preterm birth), indicating that preterm birth is classified as a specific type of gestation abnormality and also falls within the broader category of pregnancy and puerperal disorders. This dual classification underscores that preterm birth represents both an abnormality of the gestational process and a disorder affecting the pregnancy‑postpartum continuum, which is important for clinicians to recognize when assessing risk factors, planning management, and providing comprehensive care to patients experiencing or at risk for early delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2842
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), and short stature (قصر القامة) is clinically associated with a threat of premature labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر). This interrelationship indicates that infants born preterm with low birth weight are more likely to develop reduced height, and that reduced height itself may signal an increased risk of threatened premature labor. Recognizing these connections can help clinicians monitor growth patterns and anticipate obstetric complications, thereby improving patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1721
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight, early or threatened labor, threatened preterm labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر), short stature (قصر القامة), and neonatal death (وفاة حديث الولادة) are interrelated clinical concepts. Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature, indicating that babies born prematurely with low weight are more likely to develop reduced adult height. Short stature itself is clinically associated with neonatal death, suggesting that children who remain short have an increased risk of dying in the newborn period. Early or threatened labor is used to classify threatened preterm labor, and threatened preterm labor is also clinically associated with short stature. Together, these relationships highlight that low birth weight and threatened preterm labor can contribute to short stature, which in turn is linked to higher neonatal mortality, underscoring the importance of monitoring growth and labor complications in perinatal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 21 -- Level 1 -- Community 196
retrieved_docs.content: The community’s knowledge base organizes pregnancy‑related maternal conditions under the umbrella term **Complications mainly related to pregnancy**, which is subdivided into the categories **Complications of pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium** and **Other conditions or status of the mother complicating pregnancy, childbirth, or the puerperium**. Specific parent concepts within this hierarchy include **Early or threatened labor**, **Hemorrhage during pregnancy** (e.g., abruptio placenta, placenta previa), **Hypertension complicating pregnancy, childbirth and the puerperium**, and the broader “other conditions” group. **Cervical shortening in pregnancy** is classified as a child of both “Other conditions or status of the mother…” and “other complications of birth; puerperium affecting management of mother,” and it is linked via an inverse‑isa relationship to **abnormalities of cervix (obstetric)**. Cervical shortening is further divided into trimester‑specific entities—**Cervical

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 1 -- Community 146
retrieved_docs.content: obstetric events. Antecedents of pre‑eclampsia and hypertension (ارتفاع ضغط الدم) are included as maternal‑obstetric risk factors, with renal revascularization surgery (جراحة إعادة التوعي الكلوي) identified as a therapeutic intervention for hypertension related to renal arterial disease. This integrated knowledge base supports precise coding, epidemiologic surveillance, risk assessment, diagnosis, management pathways, and targeted newborn‑screening strategies for clinicians caring for pregnant patients.
